mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 6.0 percent signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending expectations, triggering immediate equity risk premium compression and positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0 percent over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and new housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate profit forecasts and driving sustained equity appreciation over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation when both GDPC1 and HOUST rise simultaneously, or the joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing consumer price inflation indicates supply-side efficiency gains that compress corporate margins during inflationary periods, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive when INDPRO growth exceeds CPIAUCSL growth by more than 1.5 percentage points over a 12-month rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and financial assets indicate increasing household liquidity and consumption demand, which directly supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS month-over-month changes consistently precede negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate increase exceeding 25 basis points within a single month signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises borrowing costs, suppresses equity valuations, and generates negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances following a 25 basis point rate hike over a 15-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which accelerates wage-driven consumption and sustains positive equity momentum over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 45 percent of cases over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: New housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units annually during a rising interest rate environment signals resilient construction demand that offsets monetary headwinds, producing positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns turn negative in more than 60 percent of months where HOUST surpasses 700 thousand units during a Fed tightening phase over a 10-year sample.
